CST-452 Senior Capstone II — Grand Canyon University

FaithTrack

A web-based faith resource management system built with ASP.NET Core MVC, Entity Framework Core, and SQL Server. Authenticated users can create, organize, search, and manage faith-based resources across a fully implemented three-tier architecture.

Matthew KollarDeveloper & Project Manager
Professor Michael LandrethCST-452 Instructor
20 User Stories3 Sprints — All Complete
ASP.NET Core MVC (.NET 8)Primary Framework
➔ View on GitHub ▶ Watch Screencasts ❐ View Screenshots
Project Overview

What is FaithTrack?

FaithTrack addresses a real problem: faith-based resources like Bible study guides, sermon notes, prayer journals, and reading plans are typically scattered across apps, documents, and websites with no central place to manage them. FaithTrack brings everything into one organized, searchable system.

🔐
Secure Authentication
ASP.NET Core Identity with password hashing, session management, and CSRF protection
📚
Resource Management
Full CRUD for faith-based resources with title, category, description, and URL fields
🗂️
Category System
Create and manage custom categories to organize resources by topic or type
🔍
Search & Filter
Keyword search by title and filter resources by category for fast retrieval
👤
User Profiles
Account registration, profile management, and password change functionality
✅
Tested & Documented
Manual test cases executed for all 20 user stories with traceability matrix
Technical Foundation

Technology Stack

Every technology choice was made deliberately to align with industry-standard enterprise web development practices and the three-tier layered architecture defined in the Milestone 3 architecture plan.

⚙️
ASP.NET Core MVC (.NET 8)
Application framework with controller-based routing, Razor Views, and tag helpers
🗄️
Entity Framework Core
ORM for data access with Code First migrations and LINQ-based querying
💾
SQL Server Express / LocalDB
Relational database hosting Users, Roles, Resources, and Categories tables
🔑
ASP.NET Core Identity
Built-in authentication with PBKDF2 password hashing and cookie-based sessions
🎨
Bootstrap 5
Responsive UI component library for consistent, accessible interface design
🛠️
Visual Studio 2022
Primary IDE with Kestrel web server for local development and testing
System Design

N-Layer Architecture

FaithTrack enforces strict separation of concerns across four layers. Controllers never touch the database directly. Services never know about HTTP. Repositories are swappable without touching business logic.

Presentation
ResourceController CategoryController AccountController HomeController Razor Views (.cshtml) ResourceViewModel Bootstrap 5 UI
Application
IResourceService ResourceService ICategoryService CategoryService ASP.NET Core Identity Business Rules & Validation
Data Access
IResourceRepository ResourceRepository ICategoryRepository CategoryRepository FaithTrackDbContext EF Core Migrations
Database
Users Roles UserRoles Resources Categories SQL Server Express / LocalDB

All components wired via dependency injection in Program.cs using builder.Services.AddScoped<>(). All database operations are async throughout the stack.

Development Progress

Sprint Backlog

Development followed Agile Scrum methodology across three sprints spanning CST-451 and CST-452. All 20 user stories across all three sprints are complete.

Sprint 1 — Core System 7/7 Complete
  • ✓US-1: User Login
  • ✓US-2: Login Validation & Error Messages
  • ✓US-3: Dashboard View
  • ✓US-4: View Resources List
  • ✓US-5: Add New Resource
  • ✓US-6: Edit Existing Resource
  • ✓US-7: Delete Resource with Confirmation
Sprint 2 — Categories & Search 7/7 Complete
  • ✓US-8: View Category List
  • ✓US-9: Create New Category
  • ✓US-10: Edit Existing Category
  • ✓US-11: Delete Category with Validation
  • ✓US-12: Filter Resources by Category
  • ✓US-13: Search Resources by Keyword
  • ✓US-14: User Registration
Sprint 3 — Profile & Polish 6/6 Complete
  • ✓US-15: View User Profile
  • ✓US-16: Update Profile Information
  • ✓US-17: Change Password
  • ✓US-18: Consistent Navigation Bar
  • ✓US-19: Inline Validation Messages
  • ✓US-20: Unit Tests for CRUD & Auth

Engineering Decisions

Challenges & Solutions

Three technical challenges stood out during development. Each one reinforced a real-world software engineering principle.

01
ASP.NET Core Identity Integration
Wiring Identity into the existing layered architecture without letting authentication bleed into the service or repository layers required careful middleware ordering in Program.cs and understanding how IdentityDbContext interacts with custom entity configurations in FaithTrackDbContext.
✓ Resolved by extending IdentityDbContext<ApplicationUser> and isolating all Identity concerns within AccountController and the application layer.
02
EF Core Migration Conflicts
As the schema evolved across sprints, EF Core migration conflicts arose when code-first model changes didn't match the existing database state. Each new migration had to be carefully tested locally to avoid schema drift between development sessions.
✓ Resolved by maintaining incremental migrations, keeping a backup of database state before each migration, and using update-database consistently after every model change.
03
Category Delete Cascade Protection
Deleting a category that still had resources assigned to it would violate the ON DELETE NO ACTION foreign key constraint. The application needed to catch this at the service layer and return a meaningful error message rather than letting SQL Server throw an unhandled exception.
✓ Resolved by adding a pre-delete validation check in CategoryService that queries resource count before allowing deletion and returns a user-friendly error if resources still reference the category.
